{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "jeffry55", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11176242?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/jeffry55", "id": 11176242, "login": "jeffry55", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjExMTc2MjQy", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/jeffry55", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-49247", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": [], "cwes": [], "description": "### Impact

 The POST /ClientLog/Document endpoint accepts the Authorization header's Client and Version fields and uses them unsanitized as components of the on-disk filename when persisting client-uploaded log documents. As a result, any authenticated non-admin user can include `../` sequences in the Client field to cause Jellyfin to write attacker-controlled content to arbitrary paths reachable by the Jellyfin service user, with a forced .log suffix.

This is the same bug class as CVE-2023-30626 / GHSA-9p5f-5x8v-x65m, which received a patch in April 2023. However the fix was not properly backported to the `master` branch and thus has remained in versions since. Special care will be taken to backport this for future versions.

### Patches
This issue has been fixed in Jellyfin 10.11.10.

### Workarounds
N/A

### References

Reported by Jeffry Mozalbat (@jeffry55).

title: Potential Authenticated path traversal in /ClientLog/Document
